story_text.value:
We've been using a now-discontinued version of pi-mom from the pi monorepo for a few months now. Digby is a slackbot which you can also assign Linear issues to. It has a private (Sign In with Slack, locked to your team) wiki and can serve raw assets. It's pi, so it's self-modifying and self-improving.

This isn't enterprise-grade, super secure stuff, but really a pi session anyone can use and one can works really well. Out of the box it deploys (and redeploys itself when PRs merge) itself to AWS on a small container. Runs on Claude Sonnet on Bedrock, but can use any Bedrock model easily. If you give it a Github token it'll open PRs to your codebase on its own behalf (or whoever owns the token -- very much advise you give it its own account). It can sign you into MCPs, and open its own browsers using Browser Use. Most importantly, it's very easy to fork and extend and make your own.

We've been slowly improving a lot of the sharp edges the original pi-mom came with, to the point it's usable enough for anyone to use out of the box reliably.

Some examples of use case: We gave it our Granola (MCP) and every day it ingests customer conversations and builds out our shared wiki and posts insights to a dedicated channel. It has Claude Code and Codex binaries inside to do Real Work, and does analysis and visualisations of these. Our commercial team ship features end-to-end (against preview PRs) and can work with customers iteratively and directly. It proactively investigates issues using the Datadog CLI. Much like OpenClaw you can just tell it to adjust it's behaviour and it does, and has turned into a kind of central command center guy for keeping in touch with releases and shipping Linear tickets (which scale as a nice substrate shared across dev machines, mobile-on-the-run, and shared chat).

No support but PRs welcome of course. Hope this is of use to anyone! We've loved it as an opportunity to test the limits of Sonnet and Pi and it's become a core part of our company Get Shit Done.

title.value: Show HN: OSS Pi Agent for Slack and Linear

story_text.value:
Thought it would be cool to add a self-drawing SVG animation to my portfolio, but it took some time to figure out. anime.js has this feature, but getting it to work properly and converting an image into clean SVG paths (with the right threshold and other settings) takes some time. So I built a tool for it. You drop in a photo or logo, it converts it into SVG paths, then animates those paths so they draw themselves like a pen sketch.

Works best with illustrations, cartoons, and clean line drawings. Real-world photos are harder to convert into clean paths.

Live demo: https://img2svg-animation.vercel.app/

Open to feedback or suggestions if you have any.

title.value: Show HN: Turn any image into self-drawing SVG line art

story_text.value:
I kept seeing those ESP32 toys where you plug it to your PC / Linux and have a touch screen change when Claude Code / Cursor / Codex is asking for some permission, but I didn't want to go that far. I also missed a way to remote control the agent chat when away from the computer. I glanced at my mobile and then it hit me: why not bluetooth? BT got a decent reach, doesn't depend on WiFi (specially with AP isolation), never exposed to the internet (looking at you TailScale). 
So here's my first attempt. There are some rough edges on Linux, non-working on OSX, but we'll get there.

title.value: Show HN: PocketVeto is a Bluetooth-only AI agent remote control

hits.author: pocket-veto
hits.story_text:

BT got a decent reach, doesn't depend on WiFi (specially with AP isolation), never exposed to the internet (looking at you TailScale). 
So here's my first attempt. There are some rough edges on Linux, non-working on OSX, but we'll get there.

story_text.value:
Hi HN!, I have been using Claude Code for a couple of month now and have discovered a lot of cool plugin like career-ops that helps you search for jobs and coding dj that plays lofi music from the terminal.

Although I love using them but I always feel like they could be a lot more than what Claude can currently offer. So I built Mocca(https://github.com/valehelle/mocca-hub). It's an app where the Plugin takes center stage.

Mocca has marketplace where you can discover existing plugin and install it. Each plugin has it's own tab and file directory.

Another cool thing is Mocca has a feature called Canvas. Canvas is basically a mini browser in the app that allows the agent to be more expressive in the respond rather than relying on markdown on chat. What makes Canvas cool is that you can actually tell the agent to create a mini app and it works using existing plugin.

For example I can install coding-dj plugin and ask it to create a media player according to my mood, weather and day of time. The agent will simply know to create a mini app inside the canvas with play , pause , next button.

If you are interested to know more you can visit https://github.com/valehelle/mocca-hub . It's open source.

title.value: Show HN: Mocca – Claude Code wrapper where Plugin takes center stage

story_text.value:
The first platform in the world that combines SEO tools and the production of infographics and images. It mainly supports Blogger bloggers and personal reviews of methods and sites for making money from the Internet 
The platform offers a variety of SEO tools 
1- Start by choosing the title, writing the article, checking the quality of the article, and also checking the backlinks within the article itself 
2- Then the platform moves to another set of archiving acceleration tools, starting with creating various JSON-LD codes, creating a custom permalink, examining the codes, and cleaning the article code.
3- The third group is to create a professional infographic in 20 different languages with an automatic barcode for the article, add the site logo and link, or create images to improve the site's speed in sizes consistent with Page Speed standards and in compressed webp format to speed up the template.
It also provides a tool for creating images with artificial intelligence and adding the site logo and site name 
As well as creating professional schedules in different formats 
4- The fourth group includes tools to speed up archiving, including a tool to send ping signals to more than 200 real pure engines, as well as a tool to create backlinks and a tool to solve the problem of links being discovered but not indexed, and other tools to speed up archiving and build backlinks 
5- A set of tools for making money from the site, starting with the Google AdSense acceptance check tool, then the AdSense alternatives check tool, creating a privacy policies page, case sites, affiliate marketing, and a diverse and exclusive set of methods for making money from the Internet 
6- Providing honest and personal reviews of websites and ways to profit from the Internet 
Learn about all the tools, website reviews, and ways to profit from the Internet

title.value: Show HN: 3Emalak SEO Gaint
